name: Apple Strudel
servings: serves 4–6 | prep. time 20–30 minutes | cooking time 60 minutes

ingredients.item: Granny Smith apples, peeled and chopped

method: Add the flour, salt, egg whites, lemon juice and oil to a mixing bowl. Mix well.
Add warm water and stir the mixture well.
Lightly dust your work surface with flour; add the dough and knead to make a springy, smooth dough. Set aside in a bowl for 30 minutes to settle.
To another mixing bowl, add the apples, raisins, lemon zest, sugar, and cinnamon. Mix well. Set aside for 30 minutes.
Roll the dough to make a 20–24-inch circle. Tug the edges and cut off any rough edges.
Brush with the butter and sprinkle with the chopped nuts.
Add the apples over one edge of the dough. Roll the dough up.
After rolling it completely, seal the edges.
Preheat the oven to 350°F (177°C). Grease a baking sheet lined with parchment paper with some cooking spray or melted butter.
Add the strudel and brush with beaten egg.
Bake for 55–60 minutes until evenly brown; brush with the butter 4–5 times during cooking.
Combine the sugar and cream in a bowl. Spoon over the strudel and serve warm.
